title: Pillowy Fermented Moroccan Lentils Bissara
description: A comforting, pillowy Moroccan dish featuring fermented lentils and a hint of cocoa powder. This Jain-friendly recipe is perfect for a cozy evening meal.

ingredients.name: fermentation starter
ingredients.notes: active dry yeast or bread starter

instructions: Soak the red lentils in 4 cups of water for at least 8 hours or overnight.
Drain and rinse the lentils, then transfer them to a blender or food processor with 2 cups of water.
Blend the mixture until it is smooth and free of lumps.
Transfer the mixture to a large terracotta bowl or container and mix in the cocoa powder, olive oil, salt, black pepper, cumin, coriander, and nutmeg.
If using, add the minced onion, garlic, and ground ginger to the mixture and stir to combine.
Create a fermentation starter by mixing 1 tablespoon of active dry yeast or bread starter with 1 cup of water, then letting it sit for 24 hours.
Add the fermentation starter to the lentil mixture and mix well to combine.
Cover the bowl with a cloth and let it ferment at room temperature for 24-48 hours, stirring occasionally.
Once the fermentation process is complete, stir in the infused oil, if using.
Transfer the mixture to a serving dish and garnish with chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
Serve the Pillowy Fermented Moroccan Lentils Bissara warm or at room temperature.

Note: To infuse the oil, simply add the desired herbs or spices to a small jar with a neutral-tasting oil, such as olive or grapeseed oil, and let it sit for at least 24 hours before straining and using.
